reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome (no-GPU) end-to-end across all 5 pages, desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Source calls a REAL WebLLM path: worker.js imports createEngine from lib/webllm.js and streams via engine.chat.completions.create({stream:true}); the page gates on webGPUAdapterAvailable() before load. Verified the MLC id OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC EXISTS in @mlc-ai/web-llm prebuiltAppConfig (grep of the esm.run bundle) — vram_required_MB 1776.75, context_window_size 4096, low_resource_required true. In the no-GPU headless runner every page reached the honest 'unsupported / needs-WebGPU (no GPU adapter here)' state — NEVER a faked token (0 assistant bubbles with generated text pre-run across all 5 pages).
rubric.notes: Real streamed token output on a GPU device was not observable in this no-GPU sandbox by design (WebLLM is WebGPU-only, no WASM fallback). The load path and MLC id are verified; a GPU-device run to observe live decode + tok/s is the open item.

rubric.evidence: Expected shape is a streamed text-generation answer conditioned on a system/user/assistant message array. worker.js accumulates delta.content, posts tagged answer deltas + ttft, and the page renders them into the assistant bubble; see-inside shows decode speed (tok/s), time-to-first-token, answer tokens, context used (est.), total time. The practical page runs JSON.parse() on the answer to PROVE machine-readable structured output; the wild page runs the SAME prompt at temp 0.15 vs 1.2 to show the sampling effect; the multi-model page grounds the answer strictly in MiniLM-retrieved passages.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity of the streamed answer + JSON validity is verified in code and the wire panel; observing the exact tokens requires a GPU device (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4f16_1), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance (read in the desktop dark screenshot) lists model, MLC build OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC, runtime WebLLM/MLC (not Transformers.js), params 1B q4f16_1, openness (weights+data+code+checkpoints+logs), chat template with a real system role, backend WebGPU-only ~1.8 GB, download ~845 MB, context 4096, license Apache-2.0. Matches exactly what worker.js loads (MODEL_ID OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC via createEngine).
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure. Size ~845 MB verified against the HF MLC repo blob total (845,210,838 bytes).

rubric.evidence: Every page uses the shared createModelLoader (no bespoke Load button). WebGPU gate fires first: on the no-GPU runner the loader lands in 'unsupported' with the honest needs-WebGPU message and NO action button, and the fallback panel explains how to enable WebGPU + the ~845 MB cost + '0 MB cached of 10737 MB' storage detail — no silent download. On the multi-model page the MiniLM (WASM) loader correctly shows the 'download-required' Download state (it can run without a GPU) while the OLMo loader shows needs-WebGPU — two honest states side by side. Per-model 'Clear cached model' control is provided by the shared loader once ready.
rubric.notes: On a GPU device the state matrix (absent→Download, current→auto-init no button, partial→Re-download) is driven by the same shared loader as the other WebLLM pages; the needs-WebGPU honest gate is what this environment can prove.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present on every page), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader), webgpu-capability-honest (real adapter probe → labelled unsupported). Driven runs: 0 uncaught JS errors, 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests on all 5 pages. Generation failures set status .err with the message; Stop calls engine.interruptGenerate(); the practical page surfaces JSON-validity as a labelled ok/err status.
rubric.notes: Honest needs-WebGPU rendered on every page. Offline-cached / partial-evicted state drives need a GPU device with a warm cache (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main: system-prompt textarea, temp/top_p/max-tokens ranges with live value bind, message textarea, Send/Stop/Clear. Basics: temp + max-tokens ranges + example chips + Send. Practical: source textarea + fields input + example chips (email/review/ticket) + Extract, with live JSON.parse validity. Wild: prompt textarea + example chips + 'Run both' (cold vs hot). Multi-model: two shared loaders + corpus textarea + question + Ask, with retrieved-passage + grounded-answer panels. Every control is a real labelled element; chips/buttons ≥ ~2.4rem tap target, Send ≥44px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced via rendered screenshots (light+dark, desktop+mobile); a full keyboard-only traversal on a GPU device (tab order + visible focus, Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run every answer/output area shows only a muted placeholder; on the no-GPU runner the loader shows the honest unsupported state and NO tokens are ever synthesised (verified: 0 assistant bubbles with generated text across all 5 pages pre-run). There is no canned reply path anywhere — the answer only ever comes from engine.chat.completions.create. The openness ledger is a STATIC, clearly-labelled factual comparison of what the two release models publish ('not model output. Sources linked in References') — never presented as model output.
rubric.notes: Verified across all 5 pages: no pre-baked result, and the WebGPU gate prevents any output when the model genuinely can't run.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (no content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; chat log + answer areas aria-live=polite; textareas + inputs have
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming answer on a GPU device would further validate.

rubric.evidence: Driven matrix (responsive-check.mjs): documentElement.scrollWidth <= innerWidth+1 (no horizontal overflow) at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on the main page — desktop clean · mobile clean. My own driven pass confirmed no overflow on all 5 pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model too). Screenshots read: main (desktop dark) — at-a-glance table wraps; main (light) — see-inside stat grid + openness ledger + fallback panel legible; the ledger collapses padding under 520px. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on bubbles/flex items; grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr); the wild versus grid and multi-model loader/result grids stack to one column.
rubric.notes: Support recorded needs-review by responsive-check (automated-only signal); this hand pass reading the screenshots for legibility/tap-targets across desktop+mobile+light/dark is the agent verdict. A real touch-device pass of the sliders is a nicety.

rubric.evidence: All inference AND the streaming answer accumulation run in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js, new Worker(url,{type:'module'})) so decode never blocks the main thread; tokens post incrementally via postMessage and the UI only appends text. The multi-model page also runs MiniLM embedding in its own module worker (embed-worker.js). conformance worker-compliance=source-pass, inference-off-main-thread=pass. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while streaming on a GPU device would confirm; the off-thread workers + incremental streaming are the key mitigation, matching the reference WebLLM pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream LLM chat tokens incrementally off main thread web worker INP
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread and yield/stream incrementally so it never becomes a long task that spikes INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in models/olmo-2-webllm/worker.js (a module Web Worker); the worker posts small answer deltas + ttft that the main thread just appends. No synchronous main-thread loop over tokens; the RAG page runs MiniLM in a second module worker.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance worker-compliance + inference-off-main-thread pass; driven runs stayed responsive with 0 console errors on all 5 pages

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel sliders form controls no horizontal overflow narrow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + gap + intrinsic sizing and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content; use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr) so columns stack instead of overflowing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles + composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; the wild versus grid, multi-model loader/result grids, and see-inside stat grid use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr); glance + ledger cells overflow-wrap:anywhere with a <520px padding tighten.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (scrollWidth<=innerWidth+1 at 360x740 and 1280x800); my driven pass confirmed no overflow on all 5 pages

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcement aria-live streaming
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region, label every control, and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status; aria-live=polite chat log + answer; aria-label/label on every textarea/input/range; role=group on chip sets; the openness ledger carries role=table/row/columnheader/cell + aria-label; the caret animation is disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; DOM inspection + rendered screenshots (light+dark)

guidanceConsulted.query: textarea that grows to fit content field-sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size so composer textareas grow with input instead of scrolling.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (shared CHAT_CSS) — .composer textarea and .sysbox textarea use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size caps, so the message/system inputs grow with content on both viewports.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: CHAT_CSS in models/olmo-2-webllm/webllm-chat.js; no overflow observed on mobile

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); every page links the shared stylesheet and crumbs back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs present on all 5 pages (read in screenshots)

openQuestions: On a real WebGPU device: observe the live streamed answer, measure decode tok/s + time-to-first-token in the see-inside grid, and confirm the practical page's JSON.parse validity gate on real OLMo-2 output.
Confirm OLMo-2 Instruct's chat template is applied correctly by the WebLLM build (system/user/assistant turns) so the system prompt is honoured as shown in the wire panel.
Keyboard-only traversal + visible focus across all 5 pages on a GPU device (Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter, the range sliders, the example chips).
Drive the warm-cache auto-init (no button) + partial/evicted + offline-cached states on a GPU device with the ~845 MB model cached.

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device verification pass for olmo-2-webllm: capture live streamed decode, tok/s + ttft in the see-inside grid, the wild page's cold-vs-hot temperature contrast, and the practical page's JSON validity on real output, as retained evidence.

Add an OLMo-2 'continued pre-training' explainer demo that walks the fully-open recipe (start from a published checkpoint, point at a corpus with OLMo-core) — leaning into the distinguishing openness angle no other catalogue model has.
Add a 7B OLMo-2 (OLMo-2-1124-7B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC) page as a separate higher-capability build for GPUs with more memory, cross-linked to the 1B page.

summary: olmo-2-webllm is a strong, honest, genuinely-distinct demo: the catalogue's only FULLY OPEN model, running OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC (verified present in @mlc-ai/web-llm prebuiltAppConfig: vram 1776.75 MB, context 4096, low_resource) on WebLLM/WebGPU. Its distinguishing feature is real and made legible: an 'openness ledger' (a labelled, factual weights + data + code + checkpoints + logs comparison versus the open-weights norm) alongside a private on-device chat with sampling controls, a real system role, and a see-inside decode-stats grid. Four use-case pages — basics (streaming chat), practical (structured JSON extraction with a live JSON.parse gate), wild (cold-vs-hot temperature explorer), and multi-model (MiniLM→OLMo-2 RAG). Driven end-to-end headless (no-GPU): honest needs-WebGPU state on every page (no faked tokens, 0 assistant bubbles pre-run), 0 JS/console errors, 0 failed requests, and no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 or 360x740 across all 5 pages, light + dark both legible. Real streamed decode is verified by source + MLC-id existence; observing live tokens needs a GPU device (the high-priority follow-up). Revision 1.
